title: SSC GD Constable 2026
meta_description: SSC GD Constable 2026 — recruitment in BSF, CRPF, CISF, ITBP, SSB, NIA, and Assam Rifles. Physical standards, exam pattern, salary ₹21-25K, and training details.

hero.one_liner: Constable (GD) recruitment in Central Armed Police Forces — BSF, CRPF, CISF, ITBP, SSB, NIA, SSF, and Assam Rifles

what_is_it: SSC GD Constable (General Duty) is one of the largest recruitment drives in India, typically offering 25,000-30,000+ vacancies for Constable positions in Central Armed Police Forces (CAPFs) and other organizations. The forces include BSF (Border Security Force), CRPF (Central Reserve Police Force), CISF (Central Industrial Security Force), ITBP (Indo-Tibetan Border Police), SSB (Sashastra Seema Bal), NIA (National Investigation Agency), SSF (Secretariat Security Force), and Assam Rifles.

The exam requires only 10th pass qualification, making it accessible to a massive number of young Indians. However, it has rigorous Physical Standards Test (PST), Physical Efficiency Test (PET), and Medical Examination — candidates must be physically fit to serve in paramilitary/security forces. The Computer Based Exam tests General Intelligence, General Knowledge, Elementary Mathematics, and English/Hindi at a basic level.

Starting salary is at Pay Level 3 (₹21,700-69,100 basic pay scale). With allowances, the in-hand salary is approximately ₹21,000-25,000/month, plus free accommodation, food, uniform, medical, and other facilities during service. Additional risk allowance is paid for deployment in border areas, conflict zones, and high-altitude postings.

GD Constables undergo rigorous training (6-12 months) at respective force training centers. After training, they are posted for border guarding (BSF, ITBP, SSB), internal security and anti-terrorism (CRPF), industrial security (CISF), or investigation support (NIA). Career growth through departmental exams allows promotion to Head Constable, ASI, SI, and above.

key_details.label: Height (Male)
key_details.value: 170 cm (General/OBC). 162.5 cm (SC/ST of specific states). 175 cm for some posts.

key_details.label: Chest (Male)
key_details.value: 80 cm unexpanded, 85 cm expanded (5 cm expansion mandatory)

key_details.label: Physical Test (Male)
key_details.value: 5 km run in 24 minutes, long jump 3.5m, high jump 1.2m

key_details.label: Physical Test (Female)
key_details.value: 1.6 km run in 8.5 minutes, long jump 2.7m, high jump 0.9m

key_details.label: Selection Process
key_details.value: CBT → PST/PET → Medical Examination → Document Verification → Training

exam_pattern.description: Single paper with 4 sections. 80 questions in 60 minutes — relatively easy if well-prepared. Questions at 10th class level. No sectional time limit.

extra_sections.heading: Physical Standards and Tests — Detailed Requirements
content: Physical Standards Test (PST) — measurement based, not a test:
Male: Height 170 cm, Chest 80/85 cm. Female: Height 157 cm. Relaxations for hill areas, scheduled tribes, Gorkhas, Assam/NE states.
Physical Efficiency Test (PET) — you must complete these:
Male: 5 km run in 24 minutes (flat ground). Female: 1.6 km run in 8.5 minutes. Both: Long jump and High jump as per standards.
Medical Examination — strict medical fitness check:
Vision: Distant vision 6/6 (both eyes without glasses). Near vision: N6 (both eyes). Color vision: must distinguish red and green. No squint, no night blindness.
Hearing: Normal hearing in both ears. Able to hear forced whisper from 6 meters.
General: No knock knees, flat feet, varicose veins, piles, fractures, or deformities. No mental illness. No speech defect. Weight proportionate to height.

Common rejection reasons: Flat feet (very common), varicose veins, vision issues (even slightly below 6/6 is rejected), color blindness, underweight or overweight, knock knees. Get yourself checked by a doctor before appearing for the medical.

extra_sections.heading: Training and Life After Selection
content: Training duration: 6-12 months at the respective force's training center (e.g., BSF Training Centre, CRPF RAF Centre, CISF RPTC). Training includes: physical fitness, weapons handling, drill, law, and force-specific operational training. Food, accommodation, and uniform provided free during training.

Posting after training: BSF — border areas (India-Pakistan, India-Bangladesh). CRPF — internal security duties, anti-terrorism, law and order. CISF — airports, metro stations, government installations, nuclear facilities. ITBP — India-China border (high-altitude postings). SSB — India-Nepal, India-Bhutan border.

Career growth: Constable → Head Constable (through departmental exam, 5-7 years) → ASI (Assistant Sub Inspector) → SI (Sub Inspector) → Inspector. Many GD Constables also appear for SSC CPO (Sub Inspector) exam to get direct promotion to SI rank. Some also appear for CAPFs AC (Assistant Commandant) through UPSC.

preparation_tips: The written exam is easy — 10th class level. Focus 60% of preparation time on physical fitness. Start running daily NOW — build up to 5 km in under 24 minutes. Practice long jump and high jump regularly.

For the written exam, solve 20+ previous year papers. The pattern repeats every year. Basic arithmetic (percentage, ratio, average, SI/CI), reasoning (analogies, series, coding), and GK (static + current affairs) are the main areas.

Get a full medical checkup before applying. Common disqualifying conditions like flat feet, color blindness, or vision below 6/6 cannot be fixed quickly. Know your medical status early so you don't waste time if there's an unresolvable issue.
Height and chest measurements are strictly verified. Don't rely on 'stretching' exercises to gain height. If you're borderline, check relaxation norms for your category/state.

faqs.question: Which force should I prefer?
faqs.answer: CISF is generally preferred for better postings (airports, metros, cities). BSF and ITBP involve tough border postings but offer higher risk allowance. CRPF postings are across India for internal security. NIA is prestigious but limited vacancies. Choose based on: posting preference (city vs border), allowances, and personal interest in the force's role.

faqs.question: Can girls apply for GD Constable?
faqs.answer: Yes. All CAPFs recruit female Constables through SSC GD. Physical standards are different for females (1.6 km run instead of 5 km, lower height requirement). Female constables are posted in women battalions and mixed units.

faqs.question: Is the job physically demanding?
faqs.answer: Yes. GD Constable is a physically demanding role — border patrolling, standing guard duties, riot control, convoy protection, and sometimes combat situations. You must maintain physical fitness throughout your career. Regular medical and fitness checks are conducted.

faqs.question: GD Constable vs SSC MTS — which is better?
faqs.answer: Different types of jobs. GD Constable is paramilitary — physically demanding, risky postings, but higher salary (Level 3 vs Level 1), more allowances, adventure, and faster promotions. MTS is office work — safe, routine, but lower salary and slower growth. Choose based on whether you want a physically active career or a desk job.

faqs.question: What is the training like?
faqs.answer: Training is rigorous — 5 AM to 9 PM schedule including physical training, weapons handling, drill, self-defense, law classes, and field exercises. It's similar to military basic training. You get free food, accommodation, and a training stipend. Training duration is 6-12 months depending on the force.
